@zakkster/lite-leak v1.5.0

Zero-GC leak diagnostic for the @zakkster/lite-* ecosystem.
Wraps lite-cleanup's FR primitive with owner-tree attribution and
auto-untrack via lite-signal's onCleanup. Pluggable kernel architecture.

M0 (0.1.0) = primitive layer.
M1 (0.2.x -> 0.5.0) = detection kernels. Currently at M1-b.

PUBLIC API
----------
VERSION: string
class KernelConflictError extends Error

createLeakTracker(options?) -> tracker
  options.name?: string
  options.captureStacks?: boolean
  options.onLeak?: (report) => void         // FR path terminus
  options.onError?: (err, tag) => void      // error routing (also logged)
  options.onFinding?: (finding) => void     // kernel-emitted, neutral
  options.onWarning?: (finding) => void     // kernel-emitted, pre-FR anomaly

tracker.track(target, cleanup, tag?, options?) -> handle
  options.audit?: boolean                    // opt in to owner-handle retention

tracker.untrack(handle) -> void              // idempotent, null-safe
tracker.size() -> number
tracker.registerKernel(kernel) -> disposer  // throws KernelConflictError on dup
tracker.unregisterKernel(kernel) -> void
tracker.audit() -> KernelFinding[]

// Module-level convenience:
track(target, cleanup, tag?, options?) -> handle
untrack(handle) -> void

KERNEL SHAPE
------------
{
  name: string,                                    // unique per tracker
  patchSurfaces?: string[],                        // conflict-checked
  priority?: number,                               // default 0; higher runs first
  install?(ctx: KernelContext): void,
  uninstall?(): void,
  refine?(report, record): report | null,          // FR-time classifier
  audit?(): KernelFinding[],                       // on-demand scan
}

KERNEL CONTEXT (install ctx)
----------------------------
{
  trackerName: string,
  forEachAuditedHandle(fn(handle, record)): void,  // iterate audit-opt handles
  emitWarning(finding): void,                       // -> onWarning
  emitFinding(finding): void,                       // -> onFinding
  reportError(err, tag): void,                      // -> onError + console
}

REPORT SHAPE (FR path)
---------------------
{
  tag: unknown | null,
  ownerPath: OwnerFrame[] | null,      // { id: number, kind: 'effect'|'computed'|'signal' }
  origin: string | null,
  kind: string,                        // kernel-classified or 'unknown'
  collectedAt: number,
}

SEMANTICS
---------
- track() inside effect/computed auto-registers onCleanup(untrack).
  Clean owner disposal is NOT a leak.
- Explicit untrack() cancels FR without running cleanup.
- FR path runs cleanup, then refines through kernel chain, then onLeak.
- Refine chain: first non-null wins, kernels in priority-then-registration
  order. Higher priority runs first; ties broken by registration order.
- Kernel errors route to onError + console.error, chain continues.
- audit() aggregates findings from all kernels; each also emitted via onFinding.
- audit: true opts in per-handle to owner-handle retention (dev-tool cost).

ALLOCATION NOTE
---------------
track() allocates: one {id, kind} object per owner-tree hop plus one array
for the snapshot. Well-behaved apps (shallow ownership) pay negligible cost.
BURSTY track() usage (ECS mount storms, thousands of concurrent components
inside a 16ms frame) can trigger Scavenger GC pressure. Gate lite-leak
behind a dev-only build flag in production.

KERNELS SHIPPED
---------------
createOwnerCascadeOrphanKernel()
  Detects handles whose owner tree has broken (stale / diverged /
  truncated) since track-time. Requires { audit: true }. Zero global
  state. Refines FR reports; supports on-demand audit().

  Finding shape:
    { kind: 'owner-cascade-orphan', tag, ownerPath, origin, brokenAt,
      reason: 'stale'|'diverged'|'kind-diverged'|'truncated',
      liveFrame?, handle }

createTimerOrphanKernel({ target?, warnOnNoOwner?, captureStacks?, handleRaf?, priority? })
  Patches setTimeout/clearTimeout, setInterval/clearInterval,
  requestAnimationFrame/cancelAnimationFrame on target (default globalThis).
  Wires onCleanup for owner-owned timers. Emits onWarning at set-time for
  timers set outside any owner. audit() finds currently-pending timers
  with no owner attribution or with disposed owners. Refines FR reports
  on tracked callback closures as 'timer-orphan'.

  Patch surfaces: ['setTimeout', 'setInterval', 'requestAnimationFrame']

  Warning shape (set-time):
    { kind: 'timer-orphan',
      reason: 'no-owner-set',
      timerKind: 'setTimeout' | 'setInterval' | 'requestAnimationFrame',
      timerId, origin: string | null }

  Audit finding shape:
    { kind: 'timer-orphan',
      reason: 'no-owner-pending' | 'owner-disposed-timer-pending',
      timerKind, timerId, origin }

  Refined report shape (FR-time):
    { ...report, kind: 'timer-orphan', timerKind, timerId,
      wasCleared: boolean }

HELD-VALUE CONTRACT
-------------------
Neither `cleanup` nor `tag` may close over `target`. Both are retained
on the internal record until FR fires or untrack; capturing the target
via either defeats finalization.

DEPENDENCIES
------------
Runtime: @zakkster/lite-cleanup ^1.0.0
Peer:    @zakkster/lite-signal >=1.5.0-beta.3 <2.0.0

NAMING NOTE
-----------
`untrack` collides with lite-signal's untrack(fn). Different signatures.
Alias when importing both.

REQUIREMENTS
------------
Node 20+ or browsers with FinalizationRegistry.

createListenerOrphanKernel({ EventTarget?, warnOnNoOwner?, captureStacks?, priority? })
  Patches EventTarget.prototype.addEventListener / removeEventListener.
  Auto-remove on owner cleanup via onCleanup. Emits onWarning at set-time
  for no-owner listeners. Refines FR reports on tracked listener records.
  Patch surface: ['EventTarget.addEventListener']

createObserverOrphanKernel({ target?, warnOnNoOwner?, captureStacks?, priority? })
  Replaces MutationObserver / ResizeObserver / IntersectionObserver
  constructors on target (default globalThis). Auto-disconnect() on owner
  cleanup. audit() enumerates pending observers. Refines FR reports.
  Patch surfaces: ['MutationObserver', 'ResizeObserver', 'IntersectionObserver']

createDetachedDomKernel({ root?, warnOnDetach?, captureStacks?, priority? })
  MutationObserver-based DOM-detachment detector. User opts in via
  kernel.watch(node, tag?). Emits onWarning when a watched node (or its
  ancestor) is removed from the tree without prior untrack. audit()
  walks node.isConnected for detached-at-audit findings.
  Patch surface: ['detached-dom.root']

createAsyncRetentionKernel({ target?, warnOnNoOwner?, captureStacks?, priority? })
  Replaces target.AbortController (default globalThis). Auto-abort() on
  owner cleanup. onWarning at construction outside any owner. audit() for
  pending controllers. Interoperates with lite-await's structural cleanup
  contract. Provides advise(finding) for tracker.remediate().
  Patch surface: ['AbortController']

createRafOrphanKernel({ target?, warnOnNoOwner?, warnOnRescheduleAfterDispose?, captureStacks?, priority? })
  Loop-aware requestAnimationFrame leak detection (1.1.0). Unlike
  timer-orphan (one-shot per frame), models a self-rescheduling loop as a
  chain: owner captured at first schedule is inherited by every
  continuation scheduled inside the chain's own callback (active-chain
  window, since getOwner() is undefined mid-callback). On owner dispose,
  cancels the frame armed NOW (stops the loop; timer-orphan cannot). One
  no-owner-set warning per loop, not per frame. reschedule-after-dispose
  warning for broken cascades / self-disposing loops. audit() reasons:
  no-owner-loop-armed, owner-disposed-loop-armed. refine() + advise().
  Patch surfaces: ['requestAnimationFrame', 'cancelAnimationFrame'].
  Conflicts with timer-orphan on rAF by design -- run timer-orphan with
  { handleRaf: false } to install both.

KERNELS SHIPPED IN 1.2.0
------------------------
createWorkerOrphanKernel({ target?, warnOnNoOwner?, trackObjectURLs?,
                           captureStacks?, priority? })
  Patches Worker + SharedWorker (+ URL.revokeObjectURL for bookkeeping when
  trackObjectURLs, default true). Dropping a Worker reference does NOT stop the
  thread. Auto-terminates on owner disposal.
  warning: no-owner-set
  findings: no-owner-worker-live, owner-disposed-worker-live,
            blob-url-unrevoked
  SharedWorker has no terminate(), so it is deliberately NOT auto-reaped on
  owner disposal -- audit reports owner-disposed-worker-live instead of
  reporting clean on an agent nothing can stop.
  Object URLs are ATTRIBUTED not policed: only a blob: URL passed to a worker
  constructor is recorded. Revoking right after construction is correct and is
  what @zakkster/lite-worker does.

createAudioNodeKernel({ target?, warnOnNoOwner?, trackSources?,
                        captureStacks?, priority? })
  Patches AudioNode.prototype.connect/disconnect and (trackSources, default
  true) AudioScheduledSourceNode.prototype.start/stop.
  The hook is connect(), NOT the factory methods: an unconnected node is inert
  and collectable; a connected node is retained by the audio graph.
  Full disconnect() reaps; partial disconnect(dest) does not (still audible).
  warning: no-owner-connect
  findings: no-owner-node-connected, owner-disposed-node-connected,
            source-started-not-stopped
  In-house consumer: @zakkster/lite-audio v1.1.0 (audit() empty after destroy).

createSocketOrphanKernel({ target?, warnOnNoOwner?, captureStacks?, priority? })
  Patches WebSocket + EventSource. audit() reports by readyState: a socket the
  peer already closed is NOT a leak.
  warning: no-owner-open
  findings: no-owner-socket-open, owner-disposed-socket-open
  In-house consumer: @zakkster/lite-ws.

All three hold their live registries as WeakRef behind a WeakMap, so the kernel
never pins the resource it watches and a dropped resource still reaches the FR
path for refine().

AGGREGATION (1.5.0)
-------------------
groupFindings(findings, { byOrigin? }) -> FindingGroup[]
tracker.auditGrouped({ byOrigin? })    -> FindingGroup[]
  FindingGroup = { key, kind, reason, origin, count, representative }
  key = 'kind:reason' or 'kind:reason:originHash' (FNV-1a base36), stable
  across runs of the same build.
  byOrigin default true; origins exist only with captureStacks.
  Ordered by count DESC, then key ASC (deterministic CI diffs).
  NO SEVERITY FIELD -- ranking owner-disposed-* against no-owner-* would be an
  unfalsifiable claim. count is a frequency signal and nothing more.
  Pure and tracker-independent: accepts audit() output, onFinding-collected
  findings, or a prior JSON artifact. Never throws on malformed findings
  (skipped; missing kind/reason -> 'unknown').

PRESET (1.4.0)
--------------
createDefaultKernels({ target?, warnOnNoOwner?, captureStacks?, exclude? })
  -> { kernels: Kernel[], skipped: Array<{name, reason}> }
  Composes the kernel set for the runtime. Two traps it encodes:
   1. timer-orphan claims requestAnimationFrame by default, so registering
      raf-orphan after it throws KernelConflictError. The preset always passes
      handleRaf:false so raf-orphan (the loop-aware detector) takes the surface.
      Resolving the conflict the other way silently keeps the weaker detector.
   2. A kernel whose globals are absent still registers, claims surfaces, patches
      nothing and reports clean forever. The preset checks availability first and
      returns omissions in `skipped` WITH A REASON. skipped is meant to be logged
      or asserted on, not discarded.
  NEVER includes detached-dom (needs a root) or gl-resource-orphan (needs a
  specific context). Register those explicitly.
  Options are validated fail-closed; unknown keys throw with did-you-mean.

COOKBOOK.md ships in the package: 19 recipes, 4 tiers, pinned by
test/cookbook.test.js so recipes cannot rot.

KERNEL SHIPPED IN 1.3.0
-----------------------
createGlResourceOrphanKernel({ gl, label?, warnOnNoOwner?, captureStacks?,
                               priority? })
  gl is REQUIRED (no global default; an app may hold several contexts).
  Patches create*/delete* pairs on that context object for: buffer, texture,
  framebuffer, renderbuffer, shader, program, vertexArray, sampler, query.
  Only kinds the context exposes are patched.
  A factory returning null (failed GPU allocation) is NOT tracked.
  warning: no-owner-create
  findings: no-owner-resource-live, owner-disposed-resource-live
  Findings carry resourceKind.
  audit() returns [] when gl.isContextLost() -- the driver already reclaimed
  everything, so it is not a leak. Owner disposal against a lost context is a
  no-op, not a throw.
  MULTI-CONTEXT: kernel.name and patchSurfaces are namespaced by `label`
  (auto-unique if omitted) because registerKernel enforces unique names and
  surfaces per tracker. finding.kind stays 'gl-resource-orphan' so
  auditByKind/remediate are unaffected. Same-context double install is still
  detected via the target-scoped claim.
  In-house consumer: @zakkster/lite-gl v1.3.0.

FAIL-CLOSED INPUT VALIDATION (1.2.1)
------------------------------------
Everything the tracker does not understand throws at the boundary. Rationale:
a leak detector that accepts bad config and reports clean is indistinguishable
from a clean run.

  createLeakTracker: unknown option keys throw with a did-you-mean hint.
    Known: name, captureStacks, onLeak, onError, onFinding, onWarning.
    Handlers must be functions when present (rejected at construction, NOT at
    report time).
  track(): unknown option keys throw (known: audit). Checked with a
    non-allocating for...in because track() is a hot path.
    target must be object | function | symbol; primitives throw a lite-leak
    error naming the argument.
  untrack(): identity-checked against handles this tracker issued. A foreign
    object or primitive is a NO-OP and never decrements size(). size() can
    never go negative. Owner auto-untrack routes through the same guard.
  registerKernel: a key 1-2 edits from a lifecycle hook (name, patchSurfaces,
    priority, install, uninstall, refine, audit, advise) throws as a typo.
    Underscore-prefixed keys and unrelated state are allowed. Hooks must be
    functions when present. priority must be finite. patchSurfaces must be an
    array, and declaring non-empty patchSurfaces requires install().
    Kernels with only refine()/audit() and no install() are legal.

PATCH-LIFECYCLE FINDINGS (all patching kernels)
----------------------------------------------
Patch claims are TARGET-scoped (module-level WeakMap keyed by the patch
target), not tracker-scoped: registerKernel's patchSurfaces guard only
covers one tracker, so two trackers could both wrap the same global and
neither would complain. Two reasons are emitted via onFinding by
timer-orphan, listener-orphan and async-retention:

  { kind, reason: 'patch-double-install', surfaces: string[], detail }
    Another lite-leak kernel instance already patched these surfaces on
    this target. Both are active, so events are double-counted. Emitted
    at install; deliberately NOT thrown, because installing a kernel and
    never uninstalling it is a documented, working pattern.

  { kind, reason: 'patch-layered', surfaces: string[], detail }
    At uninstall, a third party had layered a wrapper over ours. Restore
    is identity-checked, so their wrapper is LEFT IN PLACE rather than
    destroyed. Those orphaned wrappers keep delegating through the
    captured originals.

audit()/forEachAuditedHandle iterate a SNAPSHOT: a kernel may track a new
{ audit: true } handle from inside the walk without hanging it. Handles
added mid-pass are visited on the next pass.

M2 AUDIT API
------------
tracker.auditByKind(kind) -> KernelFinding[]
tracker.auditByOwner(ownerHandle) -> KernelFinding[]
tracker.remediate(finding) -> string

Kernels may implement optional advise(finding): string | null | undefined
consumed by remediate().

M2.5 SINKS
----------
createTraceSink({ tracer, leakTagPrefix?, warningTagPrefix?, findingTagPrefix?, errorTag? })
  Routes leak events into @zakkster/lite-trace as zero-duration spans.
  Default tags: lite-leak/leak/{kind}, lite-leak/warning/{kind},
  lite-leak/finding/{kind}, lite-leak/error.

createGenericSink({ onLeak?, onWarning?, onFinding?, onError? })
  Composable sink adapter. Missing callbacks are no-ops. Swallows callback throws.

createProfilerSignalSink() -> Sink + { leakCount, warningCount, findingCount,
  errorCount, lastLeakKind, lastWarningKind, reset(), dispose() }
  Reactive boundary for leak telemetry. Lifts counters into lite-signal
  signals. Batches related writes so one event = one effect run.
  Ghost-safe: ~6 signals created at construction, no per-event churn.

createStudioSink({ mount?, title?, maxLogRows?, zIndex? }) -> Sink + { mount(),
  unmount(), dispose() }
  DOM overlay in the visual style of @zakkster/lite-studio. Rolling log
  of leak events. No signals internally (ghost contract).
